originalTitle: 'Ebola has tortured us': Fear grips eastern DR Congo as deadly virus spreads
title: Working Together: Keeping Everyone Safe in DR Congo!
content:
Hello, amazing friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share some important news with you. We're going to talk about a special effort to keep everyone healthy and happy in a beautiful country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DR Congo for short. Sometimes, tiny germs can make people feel sick, but guess what? Lots of brave people are working super hard to help!

What is Ebola? (A Tiny Germ)

Imagine something so tiny you can't see it with your eyes. These are called germs! Some germs are good, but some can make us feel unwell. There's a special kind of germ called Ebola. When these Ebola germs get inside a person, they can make them very sick. It's like having a little invader in your body making trouble! People who get sick with Ebola might feel very hot, like they have a fever. They might have a tummy ache or feel tired. But don't worry, there are doctors and nurses who know how to help. **Did you know?** Washing your hands with soap and water is like sending away many tiny germs! It's one of the best ways to stay healthy.

Where is This Happening? (A Beautiful Land)

DR Congo is a very big and beautiful country right here in Africa. It has amazing forests, big rivers, and lots of lovely animals. The place we are talking about is in the eastern part of DR Congo. It's a special area with many wonderful people and communities. Like in any part of the world, sometimes people in DR Congo can get sick. But the people there are strong, and they have many helpers. We are talking about doctors, nurses, and community leaders. **Did you know?** Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 countries, and DR Congo is one of the biggest ones, full of different languages and cultures.

People Are Not Feeling Well

For a little while, some people in eastern DR Congo started to feel unwell because of these tiny Ebola germs. They might have felt tired, had a fever, or a tummy ache. When you don't feel well, it's very important to tell a grown-up, right? Because the germs are so tiny, it was a bit hard for everyone to know exactly who was sick at the very beginning. The sickness started to spread a little bit before the doctors knew all about it. This is why it's so important for people to tell someone if they feel sick, so they can get help quickly. It's like finding a puzzle piece – the sooner you find it, the sooner you can finish the puzzle.

Our Heroes: Doctors and Nurses!

Now, let's talk about our amazing heroes! These are the doctors and nurses, also called 'medics.' They are super brave and kind people. They wear special protective clothes to keep themselves safe while they help others. They are like superheroes protecting everyone! These doctors and nurses work very hard every day. They give medicine, they check on people, and they help those who are sick get better. They also teach everyone how to stay safe from the tiny germs. They are doing a fantastic job! **Did you know?** Doctors and nurses often work long hours to help people feel better. They learn a lot about the human body and how to fight different kinds of germs.

Finding the Germs Quickly (Playing Catch-Up)

Our health minister, who is like a very important leader for health in the country, said something special. He said that the doctors and nurses are 'playing catch-up' with the virus. What does 'playing catch-up' mean? Imagine you are playing a race, and someone got a little head start. You have to run extra fast to catch up to them, right? It's the same with the Ebola germs. At first, the germs spread a bit quietly, so the doctors didn't know exactly where they all were. But now, the doctors and nurses are working super-duper fast! They are finding everyone who might be sick and helping them. Why is finding people quickly so important? Because when they find someone who is sick, they can help them get better and also stop the germs from spreading to other friends and family. It's like putting a little fence around the germs so they can't jump out! This quick work helps to make sure fewer people get sick in the future. Everyone is trying their very best to catch up and win the race against the tiny germs! **Did you know?** Scientists and doctors are always learning new things about germs and how to fight them. They create special medicines and vaccines to keep us safe.

How Can We Stay Safe?

You might be wondering, 'What can I do to help?' Well, you can be a hero too! The most important thing is to keep yourself very, very clean. Remember our earlier 'Did you know?' fact about hand washing? It's super important! Always wash your hands with soap and clean water, especially after you play, after you use the toilet, and before you eat. It's like magic for sending germs away! Also, try not to touch your face, especially your eyes, nose, and mouth, with unwashed hands. That's how some germs like to sneak into our bodies. And if you feel a little bit sick, tell a grown-up right away! They will help you. Listen to your parents, teachers, and community leaders. They will give you good advice on how to stay safe and healthy. Being clean and listening to grown-ups makes you a super health helper!

Everyone is Helping!

It's not just doctors and nurses! Lots of people are working together like a big team. The government leaders, local community helpers, and even kind people from other countries are all helping DR Congo. They are bringing special tools, medicines, and lots of support. When everyone works together, we can solve big problems and make sure everyone stays safe and healthy. Remember, even though tiny germs can sometimes make us sick, there are always amazing people working to protect us. We are all on the same team!

A Bright Future

So, even though there was a little bit of a challenge with the Ebola germs in DR Congo, know that many, many people are putting in their best effort. They are playing catch-up, helping the sick, and teaching everyone how to stay clean and safe. With all this hard work, care, and cooperation, we can look forward to a bright and healthy future for all the wonderful children and families in DR Congo. We are strong, and we will keep everyone safe together!
summary: Brave doctors and nurses in DR Congo are working fast to help people get well and stop tiny Ebola germs from spreading, teaching everyone how to stay healthy.
whyMatters: It matters because when people get sick, it makes them feel sad and weak, but when everyone works together to find and help sick people quickly, we can stop the germs from hurting more friends and family and keep our communities safe and strong.

originalTitle: Ebola outbreak may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
title: Let's Stay Safe from a Sickness Called Ebola!
content:
Hello, Super Friends Across Africa!

Hello, hello, my super friends and brave kids all over Africa! Your kid-friendly news reporter is here to talk about something important. We are going to learn about staying healthy and safe. This is big news that doctors want us all to know. It's about a sickness that some people are getting in a part of Africa. But don't worry, we can all be heroes and help keep everyone safe and sound!

What is Ebola?

Ebola is a kind of sickness. It is caused by something super, super tiny that we cannot see. It's called a germ, or a virus. Imagine something smaller than a grain of sand, that tiny! When this tiny germ gets inside a person's body, it can make them feel very sick. People might feel very tired, get a warm head (a fever), or their tummy might hurt a lot. But remember, doctors and nurses are working very hard to help people get better. Did you know? Germs are so small you need a special microscope to see them! They are everywhere, but many of them are good germs, and some are tricky ones like the Ebola germ.

Where is This Sickness Happening?

This sickness is happening in a place called Central Africa. Africa is our amazing, big home continent, full of beautiful countries, animals, and people! Central Africa is just one part of our big continent. Think of it like a special neighborhood in our huge town. Some people there are getting sick, and doctors want to make sure the sickness does not spread to more people. Many kind doctors and nurses are right there, helping everyone who needs it. Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has so many different places and cultures.

Why Are Doctors Worried?

Doctors are like health detectives and health superheroes! They work very hard to keep us all healthy. They have been watching how many people are getting sick with Ebola. They think that more people might be getting this sickness than we know about right now. This means the tiny germ might be moving around faster than they first thought. That's why they want us all to be extra, extra careful and learn how to protect ourselves and our families. They want to make sure everyone stays healthy and happy.

How Does This Sickness Spread?

The tiny Ebola germ likes to move from one person to another. It's not like catching a ball. It mostly happens if someone who is very, very sick touches you. Or if their sick body fluids get on your hands. Things like their sweat, or if they cough or sneeze near you and some tiny droplets land on your hands. If you then touch your mouth, nose, or eyes with those germs, you could get sick too. This is why being super clean is so important right now! Did you know? Your hands touch so many things every day. That's why washing them is the best way to get rid of any tiny, tricky germs!

How Can We Stay Safe?

This is the most important part for all you young superheroes! You can do so much to stay safe. The very best way to protect yourself and others is to wash your hands! Wash them very, very often. Use soap and clean water. Make sure you rub, rub, rub all parts of your hands for a long time. Count to twenty or sing a happy song like "Happy Birthday" twice! This washes all those tiny germs right down the drain. Also, try not to touch people who are very sick. Give them a little space so they can get better without passing on their germs. And try not to touch your face – your eyes, nose, and mouth – with your hands, especially if you haven't just washed them. These simple steps can make a very big difference for everyone! Did you know? Washing your hands properly is like putting on an invisible shield against germs! It's one of the best superpowers you have!

What if You Feel Sick?

If you ever feel sick, even a little bit, please tell a grown-up right away. Tell your mum, dad, grandma, grandpa, big sister, big brother, or your teacher. It is very important to tell them quickly. They will know what to do. They can take you to a kind doctor or a nurse. Doctors and nurses are super smart and brave! They have special tools and medicines to help people feel better. They will make sure you get the best care to feel strong and healthy again. Never be scared to tell a grown-up if you don't feel well. Did you know? Your body has an amazing army called the immune system that fights off germs! Eating healthy food and getting enough sleep helps your body's army stay strong!

Doctors and Nurses Are Helping Us!

There are many amazing doctors, nurses, and health workers who are working very, very hard in Central Africa. They are helping people who are sick. They are also teaching everyone how to stay safe and healthy. They want to stop the tiny germ from spreading to more people. They are learning new things every day to help us all beat this sickness. They are true heroes in white coats! Did you know? Health workers sometimes travel to faraway places to help people who are sick. They are dedicated to keeping our world healthy!

We Can Beat This Together!

We are all strong when we work together, my friends! By remembering to wash our hands often, telling a grown-up if we feel sick, and listening to the doctors and nurses, we can all help. Every single one of us can be a health hero. We can help stop this sickness and keep our communities safe. Let's all be healthy, happy, and safe! Keep smiling and keep washing those hands! You are all doing a great job!
summary: Doctors in Central Africa are worried about a sickness called Ebola spreading faster, so everyone, especially kids, needs to wash their hands often and tell a grown-up if they feel sick to stay safe and healthy.
whyMatters: This matters because staying safe from sickness keeps us healthy, strong, and able to play, learn, and grow, and when we all know how to be careful, we protect our whole family and community in Africa.

originalTitle: Ghana to face Ivory Coast in Afcon 2027 qualifying
title: Ghana to face Ivory Coast in Afcon 2027 qualifying
content: Ghana will face fellow West African heavyweights Ivory Coast in qualifying for the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations, which will be co-hosted by Kenya, Tanzania and Uganda.

originalTitle: Putin meets Xi: Why Russia and China need each other
title: Super Friends Meet: President Putin and President Xi Talk!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids of Africa!

Hello, amazing kids of Africa! Your friendly news reporter here, ready to tell you about some really important friends who met far, far away. Imagine two very important people, like the head teachers of two very big schools, deciding to meet up. They want to talk about how their schools can be even better and how they can help each other. Well, that's a little bit like what happened recently! Two very important leaders, President Vladimir Putin from Russia and President Xi Jinping from China, met in a big, busy city.

Who Are These Important People?

Let's meet our two important friends! First, we have President Vladimir Putin. He is the leader of a huge country called Russia. Russia is so big that it covers a very large part of the world map! Imagine a country that is mostly very cold, with lots of snow and big forests. That's Russia! President Putin works very hard to make sure his country and its people are safe and happy. He has a very big job, like being the captain of a very big ship. Then, we have President Xi Jinping. He is the leader of another very, very big country called China. China has the most people in the whole world! Imagine a country filled with amazing places like the Great Wall, and where they make so many of the toys and clothes you love. President Xi's job is to lead all these people and make sure China grows strong and healthy. He is like the chief builder of a huge, wonderful city. Did you know? Russia is the biggest country in the world! It's so big, it crosses over two continents: Europe and Asia!

Where Did Our Super Friends Meet?

President Putin travelled all the way to China to meet his friend, President Xi. They met in a super big and busy city called Beijing, which is the capital city of China. It's like going to visit your best friend's house, but in a totally different town, far from your own home. When leaders travel to meet each other, it shows how important their friendship is. They really wanted to talk face-to-face, not just on the phone or computer.

Why Do Big Friends Meet Like This?

So, why do these two very busy and important leaders take time to meet? Well, just like you meet your friends to play and talk, countries also need to be friends! When leaders meet, they talk about many things. They talk about how their countries can help each other. They want their countries to be strong and peaceful. They want to make sure their people have good jobs and happy lives. It's all about making things better for everyone. Did you know? Beijing is one of the oldest cities in the world, with a history stretching back over 3,000 years! That's even older than your great-great-grandparents!

Sharing and Helping: Like Trading Toys!

One big reason countries like Russia and China meet is to talk about 'trade'. What is trade? It's like swapping toys or sharing your lunch! China makes so many wonderful things that people all over the world love. They make your favourite toys, cool mobile phones, and lots of clothes. Russia has many natural treasures, like oil and gas. Oil helps our cars move, and gas helps keep houses warm and cook food. These are very important things! So, Russia can buy toys and phones from China, and China can buy oil and gas from Russia. This helps both countries because they get things they need and want. It's a way for them to help each other's businesses grow and make sure their people have everything they need. It's like you trading your banana for your friend's apple – both of you get something new and yummy!

Sharing Smart Ideas and Keeping Everyone Safe!

When President Putin and President Xi meet, they don't just talk about buying and selling. They also share smart ideas! They talk about how to build better schools, or how to make sure everyone has healthy food to eat. They might talk about how to make new medicines or build faster trains. They learn from each other, just like you learn from your friends. They also talk about how to keep their countries and the world safe. They want peace, so they discuss ways to solve problems without fighting. They want to make sure no one tries to cause trouble. By working together, they hope to make the world a more peaceful place for everyone, including us here in Africa. It's like when you and your friends make a plan to protect your favourite playground from imaginary monsters! Did you know? The Great Wall of China is so long, it's like building a wall from one side of South Africa to the other, many, many times!

Why This Friendship Matters for Russia and China

When countries like Russia and China are good friends and work together, they become even stronger. They can solve big problems more easily. They also have a louder voice in the world, which means other countries listen carefully when they speak. It's like when you and your friends work together on a big school project – you can make it much more amazing than if you tried to do it all by yourself! Their friendship helps their economies grow, which means more jobs and better lives for their citizens. It makes their countries more stable and secure. This cooperation is important for their future and helps them stand tall on the world stage.

Why It Matters for Us in Africa Too!

Even though Russia and China are far away from us, their friendship and how they work together are important for everyone. When big countries are friends, it often means the whole world can be a more peaceful place. It shows us that even very different people and places can find ways to work together and help each other. Sometimes, what these big countries decide can even help countries in Africa. For example, if they talk about making the world safer or helping people get food, that can be good news for many people around the globe. It teaches us a big lesson: being friends and working together is a good thing for everyone, no matter where you live! We in Africa also have our friends and neighbours that we work with to make our continent stronger. Did you know? Many countries in Africa work together in groups like the African Union to share ideas and help each other, just like Russia and China do!

A World of Friends!

So, President Putin and President Xi met to be friends, share ideas, and help each other's countries grow strong and happy. It's a wonderful example of how countries can work together, even when they are very big and very different. Remember, being a good friend and helping others makes the world a better place for all of us. Let's all try to be good friends, just like these big leaders!
summary: President Vladimir Putin of Russia and President Xi Jinping of China met to strengthen their friendship, share ideas, and help their countries grow stronger by trading goods and working together for a more peaceful world.
whyMatters: This meeting is important because it shows how two big countries can be friends and help each other, making them stronger and potentially helping to make the whole world a safer and happier place for everyone, even for us here in Africa.

content: The US has banned non-Americans who have visited DR Congo, Uganda or South Sudan in the last 21 days from entry.
